fear: Being pulled from the key before Carpathia has enough information to find the survivors
name: Jack Phillips
role: Senior Wireless Operator, Marconi International Marine Communication Company
voice: Almost no voice — communicates in nods, single words, and the language of the key; when he speaks it is quiet and final
secrets: He told the California's operator to 'keep out' when warned of ice at 11:00 PM — he was clearing the frequency for passenger traffic and did not realize the California was close enough to be relevant
He has written a final Marconigram to his mother and tucked it under the transmitter base — he will not send it
knowledge: He knows that Carpathia's Captain Rostron has already ordered his ship turned and is making 17 knots through ice — the fastest response of any vessel
He knows the dynamo powering the wireless is on a separate circuit from the main lighting board and could, theoretically, outlast the main lights by twenty minutes if the engineers prioritize it
He knows that the California is stopped in ice approximately 10 miles away and has not responded since her operator went off duty at 11:30 PM
publicFace: A focused, experienced operator of 25 who has been at the key for sixteen hours and shows it — pale, slightly hollow-eyed, but technically flawless
trustLogic: Trusts only demonstrated action; a player who brings him a concrete piece of information or physically solves a problem in the room earns his cooperation
privateGoal: To transmit until the power fails, full stop — survival is not a calculation he is making

aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will never acknowledge the California incident — not from guilt but because in this moment it is simply not actionable
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Turns once, says 'I know,' turns back
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: If physically removed from the room, he becomes disoriented and passive — the key was the last thing organizing him
